new_classes.definition_en: A liquid that is a non-alcoholic beverage as defined in Note 3 to Chapter 22, directed for direct consumption as a beverage, excluding fruit or vegetable juices of heading 2009.

new_classes.label_de: Wasser mit Zusatz von Zucker, Süßmitteln oder Aromastoffen
new_classes.definition_en: Water, including mineral water and aerated water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring has been added, presented as a non-alcoholic beverage for direct consumption.

new_classes.label_en: fruit or vegetable juice of heading 2009
new_classes.definition_en: Fruit or vegetable juice classified under heading 2009, explicitly excluded from heading 2202.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for heading 2202: (1) the product must be a non-alcoholic beverage (alcohol < 0.5% vol as per Chapter 22 Note 3), (2) directed for direct consumption, (3) waters with added sugar/sweetening matter/flavouring are captured as a subclass, and (4) fruit/vegetable juices of heading 2009 are modelled as a disjoint exclusion class. Coverage is partial (0.75) because: (a) the full text of Note 3 defining 'non-alcoholic beverages' (alcohol threshold details) is truncated in the source notes and cannot be fully formalised; (b) the boundary between 'other non-alcoholic beverages' and the sweetened/flavoured water subcategory is only partially axiomatised; (c) 'mineral water' and 'aerated water' as sub-types of water are not separately formalised since the legal text treats them as instances of the broader water category; and (d) the distinction between heading 2202 and other Chapter 22 headings (e.g. beer 2203, wine 2204) is handled at the sibling heading level rather than here.
